commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review of the diagnostic UX/security wording and merge decision.; Cleared: The diff adds redacted diagnostics and tests without changing proxy routing, SSRF enforcement, dependency sources, workflows, secrets, or package resolution. commands.review_summary:
Adds a web_fetch proxy-env diagnostic to doctor, status, and deep security audit when HTTP(S)_PROXY is configured but tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy is not enabled.

PR surface: Source +113, Tests +450. Total +563 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main keeps web_fetch direct unless useTrustedEnvProxy is exactly true and has no matching proxy-env diagnostic in doctor/status/audit; the PR body supplies built CLI after-fix output for all three surfaces.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review of the diagnostic UX/security wording and merge decision.

target.title: fix(doctor): warn when HTTP(S)_PROXY is set without tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` (or their lowercase variants) set in the Gateway process environment see `web_fetch` silently use direct connections instead of routing through the configured proxy. On networks that block direct egress (corporate firewalls, regional proxies), this causes confusing `web_fetch` timeouts with no operator feedback about the missing `tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy` opt-in.

The `tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy` option has existed since the original landing for this surface, but there is no in-product surface that tells operators it exists or that their proxy env vars are being ignored. Users discover the option via changelogs, GitHub issues, or community channels.

Closes #95560.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds a diagnostic warning that fires in three existing surfaces — `openclaw doctor`, `openclaw status`, and `openclaw security audit --deep` — when any of `HTTP_PROXY`, `HTTPS_PROXY`, `http_proxy`, or `https_proxy` is present in the Gateway process environment but `tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy` is not enabled. The change is diagnostic-only:

- `web_fetch` routing is unchanged
- The SSRF DNS-pinning boundary in `src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ts` is unchanged
- No default config value changes
- No auto-flip of the opt-in (a hard-flip would interact poorly with the open NO_PROXY work in #93807 and was explicitly declined by the reporter)

Detection delegates to the existing per-protocol predicate `hasEnvHttpProxyConfigured("http", env) || hasEnvHttpProxyConfigured("https", env)` from `src/infra/net/proxy-env.ts:64`. This is the same predicate the `web_fetch` runtime uses (`fetch-guard.ts:507` → `shouldUseEnvHttpProxyForUrl` → `hasEnvHttpProxyConfigured`), so the warning gate matches env vars that the `web_fetch` dispatcher would actually consider:

- lowercase HTTP/HTTPS env vars take precedence over uppercase,
- an empty lowercase value intentionally shadows the uppercase value,
- `ALL_PROXY` / `all_proxy` alone do **not** fire — the per-protocol helper only reads `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `http_proxy` / `https_proxy`, and the `web_fetch` dispatcher (built with no explicit options at `fetch-guard.ts:528`) does not honor `ALL_PROXY` for HTTP/HTTPS even when `useTrustedEnvProxy` is on. Warning on `ALL_PROXY`-only env would be misleading remediation.

Prior art in this repo: this PR mirrors the shape of #74271 ([PR #74433](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/74433), `fix(doctor): warn when OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN env overrides gateway.auth.token config`) — pure resolver in `src/gateway/` + integration into the same three doctor / status / security-audit surfaces + per-surface unit tests + `severity: "warn"`. The remediation text follows the canonical SSRF framing already used by `docs/tools/web-fetch.md:169-172` and `src/media-understanding/shared.ts:432-441` so it does not contradict the project's existing security wording.

### Scope boundaries (deliberate)

- The resolver lives at `src/gateway/web-fetch-proxy-source-conflict.ts` to mirror the #74271 precedent path (`src/gateway/auth-token-source-conflict.ts`). The subject is a `tools.web.fetch.*` concern, so `src/agents/tools/` would also be a defensible location; the precedent path is kept for minimum-diff, minimum-cognitive-load review against the precedent.
- Explicit `tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy: false` also produces the warning. The runtime gate at `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts:135` is `=== true`, so `undefined` and explicit `false` route `web_fetch` direct identically; treating them the same in the diagnostic is consistent with runtime behavior. Operators who explicitly opted out for SSRF safety see one informational line per `doctor` run rather than a silent miss; this matches the precedent's "no per-finding suppression" stance.
- Explicit `tools.web.fetch.enabled: false` suppresses the warning. When the tool is disabled, `web_fetch` cannot make direct requests, so the proxy opt-in diagnostic would be misleading.
- `NO_PROXY` is intentionally out of scope. Sibling issue #93807 owns NO_PROXY handling for `useTrustedEnvProxy`; there are active PRs on that surface (#94541, #93840, #93860).

## User Impact

Operators with `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` set who run `openclaw doctor`, `openclaw status`, or `openclaw security audit --deep` see a Security-section warning that names the env var, points at `tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy`, and explains the SSRF / DNS-pinning trade-off of enabling it. Operators without proxy env vars, with the opt-in already enabled, or with `web_fetch` explicitly disabled see no new warning. There is no behavior change for `web_fetch` itself.

## Evidence

### Focused regression on the three changed surfaces

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs     src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts     src/security/audit-gateway.test.ts     src/commands/status.scan.config-shared.test.ts

src/security/audit-gateway.test.ts: 16 passed
src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts: 44 passed
src/commands/status.scan.config-shared.test.ts: 16 passed
Total: 76 passed
```

Coverage now includes the original positive/negative matrix plus the disabled-tool regression:

- positive uppercase `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY`
- positive lowercase `http_proxy` / `https_proxy`
- mixed case such as `HTTP_PROXY` + `https_proxy`
- empty-lowercase-shadows-uppercase, matching undici precedence
- `ALL_PROXY` / `all_proxy` only stays negative because the dispatcher does not honor those for this path
- `tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy: true` stays negative
- `tools.web.fetch.enabled: false` stays negative across doctor, status, and security audit

### Full built CLI proof

Built and exercised a fresh isolated checkout at PR head `8533be955956ce9c62f17b46b49b4b26024db5e2`:

```
$ corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
Done in 6.6s using pnpm v11.2.2

$ corepack pnpm build
[build-all] phase timings: total 160.8s; slowest tsdown 121.0s
```

The built output contains the disabled-tool guard:

```
$ grep -R -n "enabled === false" dist | grep web-fetch-proxy-source-conflict
dist/web-fetch-proxy-source-conflict-*.js: if (params.cfg.tools?.web?.fetch?.enabled === false) return null;
```

Then ran the built CLI entrypoint (`node dist/entry.js`) against two isolated configs with `HTTP_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:7897`.

**Default / enabled `web_fetch` config:** all three changed command surfaces report the proxy opt-in diagnostic.

```
$ node dist/entry.js doctor --non-interactive --no-workspace-suggestions
exit=0

[Security]
- WARNING: HTTP_PROXY set without tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy.
  Detected HTTP_PROXY in the process environment, but
  tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy is not enabled, so web_fetch will use
  direct connections and ignore the configured proxy. This can cause silent
  timeouts on networks that block direct egress.
  Fix: Set tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy: true in openclaw.json if
  web_fetch should route through the proxy. Note: this lets the proxy resolve
  DNS for web_fetch instead of local DNS pinning, so only enable when the
  proxy enforces outbound policy.
- Run: openclaw security audit --deep
```

```
$ node dist/entry.js status --json --timeout 1000
exit=0

"secretDiagnostics": [
  "HTTP_PROXY set without tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy: Set tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy: true in openclaw.json if web_fetch should route through the proxy. Note: this lets the proxy resolve DNS for web_fetch instead of local DNS pinning, so only enable when the proxy enforces outbound policy."
]
```

```
$ node dist/entry.js security audit --deep --json
exit=0

{
  "checkId": "tools.web.fetch.env_proxy_without_use_trusted_env_proxy",
  "severity": "warn",
  "title": "HTTP_PROXY set without tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy",
  "detail": "Detected HTTP_PROXY in the process environment, but tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy is not enabled, so web_fetch will use direct connections and ignore the configured proxy. This can cause silent timeouts on networks that block direct egress."
}
```

**Disabled `web_fetch` config (`tools.web.fetch.enabled: false`):** the same built CLI commands complete and do not emit the proxy opt-in diagnostic.

```
$ node dist/entry.js doctor --non-interactive --no-workspace-suggestions
exit=0

Target-string check over stdout/stderr:
- "HTTP_PROXY set without tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy": absent
- "tools.web.fetch.useTrustedEnvProxy is not enabled": absent
- "web_fetch will use direct connections": absent
```

```
$ node dist/entry.js status --json --timeout 1000
exit=0

"secretDiagnostics": []
```

```
$ node dist/entry.js security audit --deep --json
exit=0

matching findings for "tools.web.fetch.env_proxy_without_use_trusted_env_proxy": []
```

### Local gates

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`: clean
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/web-fetch-proxy-source-conflict.ts src/commands/doctor-security.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.config-shared.test.ts src/security/audit-gateway.test.ts`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`: exit 0
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`: exit 0

### What was not tested

- A packaged npm/tarball install path. The proof above builds this PR head from source and runs the built `dist/entry.js` CLI.
- Interaction with `NO_PROXY` (sibling #93807 owns that surface).

## Risks / Mitigations

- **Risk:** Diagnostic noise for operators who explicitly set `useTrustedEnvProxy: false`. **Mitigation:** Matches runtime semantics (`=== true` gate); message is informational and points at the trade-off.
- **Risk:** The resolver lives under `src/gateway/` but its subject is `tools.web.fetch.*`. **Mitigation:** Mirrors the #74271 precedent path for ease of review. Easy to move in a follow-up if the maintainer prefers a different placement.
- **Risk:** Accidentally suppressing the diagnostic for normal proxy-env users. **Mitigation:** Existing positive coverage remains across doctor, status, and security audit; the new guard only applies when `tools.web.fetch.enabled === false`.
target.author: nxmxbbd
